titleStmt.title: Letter from Graham Roger to Matthew Nathan, 20 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Graham Roger

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Graham Roger, assistant secretary to Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) who was under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.
Roger acknowledges a letter received regarding a proposed amendment to the Tralee and Dingle Committee management order 1896. It notes that the Lord Lieutenant , Ivor Guest Lord Wimborne (1873-1939), should be informed that the Board of Trade have no observations on the matter.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5613/7215

correspAction.placeName: 7, Whitehall Gardens, London, S. W. England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
BOARD OF TRADE
( RAILWAY DEPARTMENT ),
7, WHITEHAL GARDENS,
London S.W. 20th April, 1916. Sir, In reply to your letter of the 6th April, ( No. 4339 )
forwarding draft of an Order proposed to be made amending
the Tralee and Dingle ( Committee of Management ) Order 1896
I am directed by the Board of Trade, to state for the in-
formation of the Lord Lieutenant, that they have no observa-
tions to offer in the matter. I am,
Sir
Your Obedient Servant, Graham Roger The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle.
